name: Santa Bárbara Castle and Serra Grossa Loop
name_original: Running por el Castillo de Santa Barbara y la Serra Grossa de Alicante
description: This difficult 6.3-mile (10.2 km) jogging route combines urban exploration with natural landscapes and historic landmarks in Alicante.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Alicante offer a diverse range of terrains, from scenic coastal paths to challenging mountain trails. The region is characterized by its Mediterranean coastline, urban green spaces, and surrounding hills, providing varied landscapes for runners. This combination of features, alongside a climate conducive to year-round outdoor activity, makes Alicante a notable destination for running enthusiasts. Best jogging routes around Alicante The most popular jogging route is Running by the Castle of Santa Barbara and the Serra Grossa of Alicante, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the historic castle and coastal hills. Another top favourite among local runners is Santa Cruz Neighborhood – Explanada de España Promenade loop from Mercado, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This route combines urban exploration with the scenic Explanada de España promenade. Local runners also love the Alicante Promenade and Marina – Parque de Canelejas loop from l'Albufereta, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through coastal promenades and urban parks, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Alicante is defined by its coastal paths, urban green spaces, and surrounding hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from relaxed jogs to more challenging trail runs. The routes in Alicante are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 8500 runners have used komoot to explore Alicante's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alicante?
A: Alicante offers a wide selection of over 200 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include everything from coastal paths to challenging mountain trails.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes in Alicante for beginners or casual runners?
A: Yes, Alicante has approximately 20 easy jogging routes. Many of these are found along the scenic promenades and urban green spaces, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Explanada de España Promenade loop from Mercado&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.7 km path perfect for a relaxed run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Alicante's running trails?
A: You'll find a diverse range of terrains in Alicante. The region features picturesque coastal paths, flat urban promenades like the San Juan Beach Promenade, and more challenging mountain trails in areas such as Serra Gelada Natural Park or Montgo Mountain. This variety ensures options for all types of runners.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging paths in Alicante?
A: Absolutely. For families, routes along the flat, accessible promenades are ideal. El Palmeral Park also offers a tranquil setting with lush greenery, suitable for a leisurely jog with children. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38261466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cruz Neighborhood – Explanada de España Promenade loop from Mercado&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.8 km path that combines urban exploration with a scenic, family-friendly promenade.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Alicante province?
A: Yes, many of Alicante's running routes are designed as loops, offering convenient circular options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31424579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante Promenade and Marina – Parque de Canelejas loop from l'Albufereta&lt;/a&gt; is a 11.9 km trail that takes you through coastal promenades and urban parks, providing a great circular experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see while running in Alicante?
A: Alicante's routes often feature stunning views and landmarks. You can run past the historic Santa Barbara Castle, along the iconic Explanada de España, or enjoy coastal vistas. For natural beauty, consider routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2652282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Grossa&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the diverse landscapes of Serra Gelada Natural Park. The region also boasts unique attractions like the Algar Waterfalls and El Hondo Natural Park, which are great for exploring after a run.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Alicante?
A: Many natural parks and coastal paths in Alicante are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you follow local regulations. Areas like the Serra del Maigmó offer extensive trails where you and your canine companion can enjoy the outdoors together. Always check specific park rules before heading out.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alicante?
A: The komoot community highly rates Alicante's running routes, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from stunning coastal views to challenging mountain trails, and the pleasant Mediterranean climate that makes year-round running enjoyable. The variety of routes for all skill levels is also a common highlight.

Q: Are there challenging trail running options near Alicante?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Alicante's surrounding hills and natural parks offer excellent trail running opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872768459/running-by-the-castle-of-santa-barbara-and-the-serra-grossa-of-alicante" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running by the Castle of Santa Barbara and the Serra Grossa of Alicante&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.2 km trail with significant elevation gain. Serra Gelada Natural Park and Montgo Mountain also provide rugged landscapes and challenging inclines for experienced trail runners.

Q: Are there jogging routes in Alicante that offer great viewpoints?
A: Yes, many routes in Alicante provide spectacular viewpoints. Running along the coast offers constant sea views, and trails leading up to areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2652282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Grossa&lt;/a&gt; or within Serra Gelada Natural Park will reward you with panoramic vistas of the Mediterranean and the city. The route around Santa Barbara Castle also provides elevated perspectives.

Q: What is the best time of year to jog in Alicante?
A: Alicante's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for jogging almost year-round. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in winter, mild conditions prevail, while early mornings or late evenings are best for summer runs to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there running routes accessible by public transport in Alicante?
A: Many urban and coastal running routes in Alicante are easily accessible via public transport. The city's tram and bus networks connect well to popular starting points along the promenades and near parks, making it convenient to reach your chosen running path without a car.

Q: Where can I find parking for jogging trails in Alicante?
A: Parking availability varies by location. For urban routes along the Explanada de España or near El Palmeral Park, public parking garages or street parking might be available. For trails in natural parks like Serra Gelada or Font Roja, designated parking areas are usually provided at trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information.
tips: Santa Bárbara Castle offers a magnificent view over Alicante and the sparkling Mediterranean Sea. 💦 From up here, the city, harbor, and coastline lie at your feet, and on a clear day, the view extends far across the bay. A place that combines history, vastness, and sea air – and is definitely worth a visit. 👌

The Serra Grossa offers an ideal place to practice trail like hiking just a few meters from the center of the city of Alicante. It has countless trails and variants, each with its spectacular views of the coast and the city.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this running route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult run. It's approximately 10.2 kilometers long with an elevation gain of about 330 meters. You'll need very good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes, as some sections can be quite demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this running route?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies. The path leading up to Santa Bárbara Castle generally consists of well-made gravel and rock, and can be quite steep. In the Serra Grossa area, you'll find a forest park with Mediterranean vegetation. While some parts are easier, alternative routes in Serra Grossa can involve scrambling and even rope assistance.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning panoramic views. You'll pass by the historic View from Santa Bárbara Castle, providing continuous vistas of Alicante city and the bay. From various points along Serra Grossa, you can enjoy expansive views of Alicante city, the Mediterranean Sea, the castle, and the beaches of La Albufereta.

faqs.question: Is this running route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations can vary, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in natural areas like Serra Grossa, often requiring them to be on a leash. For the Santa Bárbara Castle area, it's best to check local signage or official Alicante tourism information regarding pet access to specific castle grounds or pedestrian paths.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: Alicante enjoys a Mediterranean climate. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months. Early mornings are often ideal to beat the heat and enjoy quieter trails.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this running route?
faqs.answer: Parking options near the starting points of this route, such as around the base of Santa Bárbara Castle or near the access points to Serra Grossa, can include street parking or public parking garages in Alicante city. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this running route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need permits or have to pay entrance fees to run on this route, which includes the paths around Santa Bárbara Castle and through Serra Grossa. These are public access areas.

faqs.question: Does this running route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of "El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana" and also utilizes parts of "Passeig de la Pedrera," "Túnel de acceso ascensor al Castillo de Santa Bárbara," "Passeig de la Serra Grossa," and "Via Verda de la Cantera."

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: Given its distance of approximately 10.2 kilometers and a difficult rating, the estimated duration for jogging is around 1 hour and 22 minutes. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is primarily natural and historical, being close to Alicante city means amenities are accessible. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms in the urban areas near the base of Santa Bárbara Castle and within Alicante city center. It's recommended to carry water and any necessary snacks, especially when on the more secluded sections of Serra Grossa.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its classification as a difficult run with steep sections and varied terrain, including potential scrambling in Serra Grossa, the full 10.2 km route is generally not recommended for families with young children or those seeking an easy stroll. Shorter, less demanding sections around the castle or within Serra Grossa might be suitable for walking, but the combined running route requires a good level of fitness and sure-footedness.
